speed_limits.notes: Posted signs override defaults. Lower limits can apply in tunnels, roadworks, residential zones, and environmental speed-control areas.

alcohol_limit.notes: A lower 0.01% BAC limit applies to novice drivers and some professional drivers.

rules.seatbelt_rules: Seatbelts are mandatory for all occupants.
rules.child_seat_rules: Children under 14 years and shorter than 135 cm need an appropriate child restraint.
rules.phone_usage_rules: Handheld phone use is prohibited while driving. Hands-free systems are allowed.
rules.headlights_rules: Use headlights at night, in tunnels, and when visibility is poor. Motorcycles must use lights during the day.
rules.priority_rules: Drive on the right. Right-before-left applies at unmarked intersections unless signs or road markings say otherwise.

tolls.notes: Motorways and expressways require a valid vignette or digital toll. Some Alpine tunnels and passes have additional special tolls.

rules.parking_rules: Short-stay parking zones are common in cities and are strictly enforced. Check local signs and digital parking rules.
rules.winter_rules: From 1 November to 15 April, winter tyres are required in winter road conditions. Snow chains may be required where signed.

summary: Austria is generally straightforward for road trips, but motorways require a vignette and mountain weather can change driving conditions quickly. Expect strict speed, parking, and winter-equipment enforcement, especially around cities and Alpine routes.
common_traps: Driving on motorways or expressways without a valid vignette or digital toll.
Special toll roads in Alpine tunnels and passes are separate from the normal vignette.
Winter tyres are required in winter road conditions from 1 November to 15 April.
City parking zones can be confusing and are enforced quickly.
rental_and_idp_notes: EU/EEA licences are generally accepted. Non-EU drivers should check rental-company requirements; an International Driving Permit is recommended and may be required depending on the licence.

regional_variations.notes: Short-stay parking applies citywide in many areas. Use official signs, parking apps, or public transport where possible.
Mountain passes, snow chains, seasonal closures, and special toll roads are common. Check route conditions before driving.
